overview.description: AI-powered clinical risk detection and patient safety applications for hospitals
overview.description_long: Datlowe (s.r.o.) is a Prague-based healthtech company combining AI, data science, and deep clinical expertise to help hospitals detect clinical risks earlier and prevent costly adverse events. Their flagship product HAIDI automates healthcare-associated infection (HAI) surveillance by continuously analyzing hospital data — clinical notes, lab results, and patient records — to flag emerging risks in real time. Their second product MERIE is a virtual assistant for medication risk prevention that scans patient records to detect pharmacotherapy risks. Datlowe solutions integrate with hospital IT infrastructure via on-premise deployment, support FHIR and SNOMED CT standards, and are fully GDPR compliant. The company serves 55+ hospitals across Central and Eastern Europe and reduces manual review time by up to 90%.

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague (LinkedIn: 2014, website: 'since 2017'). Built AI and NLP applications for the healthcare sector, starting with infection surveillance and expanding to medication risk prevention. Grown to serve 55+ hospitals across Central and Eastern Europe.

key_milestones.event: Company describes itself as active since this year in building healthcare AI solutions
Serving 55+ hospitals across Central and Eastern Europe with HAIDI and MERIE products

metadata.notes: Founded year discrepancy: LinkedIn says 2014, website says 'Since 2017'. Leadership roles not confirmed — Jan Kavalirek and Jakub Kozák appeared in a Czech TV discussion about Datlowe but specific roles unconfirmed. Company has two Prague office addresses listed on website. No careers page found. No Glassdoor data available.
